tools.name: flake8
tools.scope: Python code in validation/ directory (not enforced in CI)

linting.notes: CI enforces Helm linting and link checking on PRs. Python linting is available but not enforced in CI.

testing.framework: Bash conformance tests + Python preflight validation
env_vars: KUBECONFIG - Path to Kubernetes config file
NAMESPACE - Target namespace for conformance tests (default: llm-d)
PROFILE - Deployment profile to validate (default: kserve-basic)
testing.notes: Tests are integration tests that require a live Kubernetes cluster with deployed services. Cannot run in isolation. The validation/ directory contains a containerized preflight check tool that validates cluster readiness.

gating_checks.name: Verify registry.redhat.io images (kserve-ci)
gating_checks.command: helm template test charts/kserve | grep -oE '(quay\.io|ghcr\.io|docker\.io)[^"'\''[:space:]]+' || true

gating_checks.name: Verify webhook annotations (kserve-ci)
gating_checks.command: helm template test charts/kserve | grep -A20 'kind: ValidatingWebhookConfiguration\|kind: MutatingWebhookConfiguration'

ci.notes: CI is focused on syntax validation (Helm, links) not functional testing. kserve-release.yaml and docs.yml run on push to main, not on PRs. No tests require cluster access in CI.

build.install_command: None (infrastructure deployment, no dependencies to install for development)
build.build_command: None (Helm charts are declarative, no build step)
build.notes: This is an infrastructure deployment repository. The 'build' process is deploying to a Kubernetes cluster via 'make deploy-all'. For validation tool: cd validation && make image

conventions.notes: This is an infrastructure/deployment repository, not a traditional software project. Tests are deployment validation scripts that require live infrastructure.

setup_commands: apk add --no-cache bash git curl helm python3 py3-pip make

lint_commands.command: bash -n scripts/cleanup.sh && bash -n scripts/setup-gateway.sh && bash -n scripts/collect-debug-info.sh
lint_commands.notes: PASS - All utility scripts have valid syntax

test_commands.notes: REQUIRES INFRASTRUCTURE - Cannot validate without a running Kubernetes cluster with deployed KServe stack
PASS - Script executes and shows help. Full validation requires cluster access.

container_recipe.notes: This repository contains infrastructure deployment code, not traditional software. Linting validates Helm chart syntax and bash/python script syntax. Functional tests require a live Kubernetes cluster with deployed operators and are not runnable in isolation.

results.command: apk add bash git curl helm python3 py3-pip make && pip install --break-system-packages configargparse kubernetes
results.output_snippet: Successfully installed certifi-2026.2.25 charset_normalizer-3.4.6 configargparse-1.7.5 durationpy-0.10 idna-3.11 kubernetes-35.0.0

==> Linting .
[INFO] Chart.yaml: icon is recommended

1 chart(s) linted, 0 chart(s) failed
results.notes: Helm lint passed for kserve chart
Script executes correctly, but actual tests require Kubernetes cluster

validation.summary: install OK, lint OK (all charts pass), bash syntax OK, python syntax OK. Tests require Kubernetes infrastructure.

gaps: No unit tests - all tests are integration tests requiring live Kubernetes cluster
Conformance test (test/conformance/verify-llm-d-deployment.sh) requires deployed KServe infrastructure
Python validation tool requires cluster access to run actual checks
Link checking (lychee) not validated - requires separate tool installation
No automated way to validate Makefile deployment targets without cluster access
No mocking or stubbing infrastructure for isolated testing
CI only validates syntax, not functionality
